rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior marketing research on how content type affects outcomes, and the cited paper is a closely related causal text-content study linking specific review content dimensions to conversion. Both focus on theory-driven textual content coding and downstream behavioral outcomes, so this could be a substantive prior-work anchor rather than a perfunctory mention. With no local citation context provided to prove it is incidental, it should be retained for full judgment.
